title: rosa gallica subsp. leiostyla
intro: Rosa gallica subsp. leiostyla, a subspecies of the Rosa gallica, is a type of wild rose that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa gallica subsp. leiostyla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Rosa gallica subsp. leiostyla is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
